title: PCR S11 - Red Sea Medium Preparation
author: Roscoff Culture Collection
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sz3ef8n

steps.instruction: We generally prepare two or three 10L carboys at a time To 1 L of H 2 O, add 33.33g of Red Sea Salt Dissolve by shaking (20 min on agitator) Heat seawater during 20min at 100°C Red Sea Salt Red Sea

Add to seawater under laminar flow hood the following nutriments that have been autoclaved (except for vitamin) In Roscoff, we generally also add 1 mL NaNO 3 1M for Synechococcus (useless for Prochlorococcus but it does not affect the growth) %s %s a - The original recipe is 8 µM (Rippka et al 2000 IJSEM 50, 1833–1847). But it works fine for Prochlorococcus ... b - If one adds 0.1 mL par L, the final concentration of Gaffron in the PCR-S11 medium is actually twice more than in the original PCR-S11 recipe. c - 10 times less than in the original receipe. Again this seems sufficient...
Filter the medium on 0.2 micron filter
Hepes-NaOH 1M To 250 mL of H 2 0, add gradually 119.15g of Hepes. Adjust pH at 7.5 and complete the volume at 500 mL. Store in refrigerator.

Na2-EDTA/FeCl3 To 40 mL of HCl 0.1 N, add gradually 1,080 g of FeCl3 To 40 mL of NaOH 0.1 N, add gradually 1,488 g of Na2-EDTA Mix both solutions Complete final volume to 2 L of sterile water Store in refrigerator

Sodium Phosphate Prepare two solutions : Monosodium dihydrogen phosphate (NaH2PO4) at 50 mM (6 g in 1 L) Disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPo4) at 50 mM (3.55 g in 500 mL) Make an equimolar mixture of these two solutions and adjust the pH at 7,5

To 500 mL of H 2 0, add gradually elements indicated in the table below. Complement the volume to 1L. Store in refrigerator. %s %s a -The original receipe uses 300 nM H 3 BO 3 final b - 4.94 in the original receipe c - 1.52 in the original receipe
